title: fix: no-play argument for API client
body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

step_plan.summary: Fixes the API client CLI so users can disable audio playback by adding `--no-play` via `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction`, while preserving the existing default behavior of playing audio.

steps.operation: Switch --play CLI flag to argparse.BooleanOptionalAction to enable --no-play and correct boolean parsing
steps.reason: The current definition (`type=bool, default=True`) makes `--play` effectively always True (e.g., `--play False` still parses truthy) and provides no supported way to disable playback; using `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` preserves default=True, keeps `--play` working, and automatically adds `--no-play` to set `args.play=False`.
steps.side_effects: Requires Python 3.9+ for BooleanOptionalAction; if the project supports older Python versions, a fallback (two flags or custom action) would be needed. CLI help output will change to include `--no-play`. Any documentation/shell completions depending on `--help` output may need regeneration. Manual smoke tests should confirm default behavior unchanged and that `--no-play` prevents the playback branch from running.
